sorting-direction.label: Sorting Direction (ASC, DESC)
sorting-direction.summary: Control sort order with ASC and DESC keywords.

sketch-asc-desc-basics.title: ASC and DESC: Sorting Basics
sketch-asc-desc-basics.bodyMarkdown:
In SQL, you can control the order of your query results using the `ORDER BY` clause. By default, results are sorted in ascending order (from smallest to largest, or A to Z). To specify the direction, use `ASC` for ascending or `DESC` for descending.

**Example:**

```sql
SELECT name, price FROM products
ORDER BY price ASC;
```
This lists products from the lowest to the highest price.

To reverse the order:

```sql
SELECT name, price FROM products
ORDER BY price DESC;
```
This lists products from the highest to the lowest price.

sketch-multi-column-sorting.title: Sorting by Multiple Columns
sketch-multi-column-sorting.bodyMarkdown:
You can sort by more than one column by listing them in the `ORDER BY` clause, each with its own direction.

**Example:**

```sql
SELECT name, category, price FROM products
ORDER BY category ASC, price DESC;
```
This sorts products alphabetically by category, and within each category, lists the most expensive items first.

quiz-single-choice-direction.title: Default Sorting Direction
quiz-single-choice-direction.prompt: What is the default sorting direction when you use ORDER BY in SQL without specifying ASC or DESC?
quiz-single-choice-direction.hint: Think about what happens if you just use ORDER BY column_name.

help.concept: ORDER BY sorts results in ascending order by default unless DESC is specified.
help.hint_1: If you don't specify ASC or DESC, SQL chooses one direction by default.
help.hint_2: Ascending order is the standard default for ORDER BY.

quiz-multi-choice-asc-desc.title: Understanding ASC and DESC
quiz-multi-choice-asc-desc.prompt: Which statements about ASC and DESC are correct?
quiz-multi-choice-asc-desc.hint: Consider what each keyword does and what the default is.

help.concept: ASC and DESC control the direction of sorting. ASC is ascending, DESC is descending. ASC is optional because it's the default.
help.hint_1: ASC means ascending, DESC means descending.
help.hint_2: You don't have to write ASC for ascending order, but you must write DESC for descending.

options.a: ASC sorts values from lowest to highest.
options.b: DESC sorts values from highest to lowest.
options.c: ASC is required for ascending order.
options.d: DESC is the default sorting direction.

quiz-drag-reorder-price.title: Order by Price DESC
quiz-drag-reorder-price.prompt: Arrange these product prices as they would appear when sorted by `ORDER BY price DESC`.
quiz-drag-reorder-price.hint: DESC means highest values come first.

help.concept: DESC sorts from highest to lowest value.
help.hint_1: Start with the largest price and go down.
help.hint_2: Put $129.00 first, then $39.99, and so on.

quiz-fill-blank-choice-keyword.title: Fill in the Sorting Keyword
quiz-fill-blank-choice-keyword.prompt:
Complete the SQL statement to sort products from newest to oldest by their `created_at` date:

```sql
SELECT name, created_at FROM products
ORDER BY created_at [___];
```
quiz-fill-blank-choice-keyword.hint: Which keyword sorts from newest to oldest?

help.concept: the missing term is used to sort in descending order, which puts the newest dates first.
help.hint_1: Descending order is needed for newest to oldest.
help.hint_2: Use the missing term to reverse the default ascending order.

quiz-fill-blank-choice-keyword.template:
SELECT name, created_at FROM products
ORDER BY created_at [___];

quiz-code-input-multi-column.title: Sort by Category ASC, Price DESC
quiz-code-input-multi-column.prompt: Write a SQL query to select the name, category, and price of all products, sorted alphabetically by category (A-Z), and within each category, from highest to lowest price.
quiz-code-input-multi-column.hint: Use ORDER BY with both columns and specify directions.

help.concept: You can sort by multiple columns by listing them in ORDER BY, each with ASC or DESC as needed.
help.hint_1: First sort by category ascending, then by price descending.
help.hint_2: ORDER BY category ASC, price DESC will achieve the desired order.
